immutable_core.semantic_intent: source-recorded urinalysis protein ordinal result event
immutable_core.semantic_definition: An event-level urinalysis protein result retaining a normalized ordinal dipstick-result domain with raw source result provenance and best-available encounter/stay linkage.
immutable_core.semantic_grain: one retained source-recorded urinalysis protein result event
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_protein_urinalysis_result must preserve source-recorded urinalysis protein ordinal result semantics and must not collapse into quantitative urine protein measurement, protein/creatinine ratio, kidney phenotype, diagnosis code truth, or absence-of-row-as-negative logic.

ordinal_result_contract.target_entity_grain: laboratory result event with best-available admission and ICU-stay linkage
ordinal_result_contract.result_domain_rule: retain only normalized urinalysis dipstick protein ordinal states: negative, trace, mild_positive, moderate_positive, marked_positive, severe_positive
ordinal_result_contract.raw_result_retention_rule: retain raw value, numeric value, itemid, label, specimen, unit, and abnormal flag provenance for audit
ordinal_result_contract.normalization_rule: map approved negative, trace, and semiquantitative mg/dL source variants to the governed ordinal dipstick domain; exclude blank placeholders and artifact numeric variants rather than treating them as negative
ordinal_result_contract.no_row_interpretation: no retained interpretable protein urinalysis result under the approved source rule; this does not prove protein negativity or absence of kidney disease

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_protein_urinalysis_result outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.
